questions.question: Rennie Boy believes that everything that happens in this world has a cause or a reason. What scientific attitude is seen in his actions?
questions.explanation: Believing that everything has a cause or reason reflects a firm set of beliefs about the order and predictability of the natural world, which is a fundamental scientific attitude.

questions.question: ____ it never recycled any waste materials, the company's environmental record was not perfect.
questions.explanation: "In that" is used to introduce a reason or explanation for a preceding statement, indicating that the company's imperfect record is due to its failure to recycle.

questions.question: Vascular tissue that transports water in leaves connects directly to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Xylem is the primary vascular tissue responsible for transporting water and dissolved minerals from the roots, through the stem, and into the leaves.

questions.question: Why do mothers usually soak meat in pineapple juice before cooking it?

choices.C: It improves the taste of meat.
choices.D: It preserves the natural taste of meat.

questions.explanation: Pineapple juice contains the enzyme bromelain, which breaks down proteins in meat, effectively tenderizing it.

questions.question: Most asteroids are found between the two planets:
questions.explanation: The main asteroid belt, where the majority of asteroids in our solar system are located, lies between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.

questions.question: Which among the following represents the smallest unit of life?
questions.explanation: The cell is universally recognized as the fundamental structural and functional unit of all known living organisms, making it the smallest unit that can be considered alive.

questions.question: Which enables the computer to communicate with other computers over the telephone?
questions.explanation: A modem (modulator-demodulator) converts digital signals from a computer into analog signals for transmission over telephone lines, and vice versa.

questions.question: Which of the following is expressed clearly, effectively states the idea, and has no structural error?

choices.A: I learned the distance of the sun from the earth was 90 million miles.
choices.B: I learned that the sun was 90 million miles from the earth.
choices.C: I learned that the sun is 90 million miles from the earth.
choices.D: I learned that the earth and the sun are 90 million miles apart.

questions.explanation: Option C uses the present tense 'is' for a general, unchanging fact, making it grammatically correct and the clearest statement.

questions.question: Basically, the Philippine agrarian reform is a question of ____
questions.explanation: Agrarian reform in the Philippines primarily focuses on the redistribution of agricultural lands to landless farmers and farmworkers.

questions.question: Which is the "brain" of a computer?
questions.explanation: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is often referred to as the 'brain' of the computer because it performs most of the processing and calculations.

questions.question: Which one of the following is an appropriate routine to shut down a non-responding application?

choices.A: Close all open documents and press the power button until the computer automatically switches off.
choices.B: Press "Ctrl + Alt + Delete", then select the non- responding application in the Task Manager window and click "End Task".
choices.C: Pull out the power cable from the back of the computer.
choices.D: Click "Shut Down" on the on the Start menu and choose "Shut down" from the Shut Down Windows drop-down list.

questions.explanation: Using Ctrl+Alt+Delete to access the Task Manager and end a non-responding application is the standard and safest way to close a frozen program without restarting the entire system.

questions.question: Why do fisherfolks catch more fish during the new moon than during the full moon?

choices.A: Fishes are more active during the new moon because of the salt content of water is just right.
choices.B: Fishes look for mates during the new moon.
choices.C: Fishes go near the surface during the new moon because the water is warmer.
choices.D: Fishes are attracted to the light from the fishing boat which fishermen make use during the new moon.

questions.explanation: During a new moon, the night sky is darker, making artificial lights from fishing boats more effective at attracting fish, which are drawn to light.

questions.question: Which is the method of growing vegetables and low plants in a nutrient-packed aerated solution with no soil at all?
questions.explanation: Hydroponics is a method of growing plants using mineral nutrient solutions in water, without soil.

questions.question: Which part of the brain is responsible for memory and intelligence?
questions.explanation: The cerebrum is the largest part of the brain and is responsible for higher-level functions such as thought, memory, language, and voluntary movement.

questions.question: Adolfo does not allow his feelings and biases to influence his recording of observations, interpretation of data, and formulation of conclusions. What scientific attitude is reflected in his actions?
questions.explanation: Objectivity in science means making observations and drawing conclusions based on factual evidence, free from personal feelings, biases, or prejudices.

questions.question: Which method of reproduction provides for the most variety of offspring?
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction involves the combination of genetic material from two parents, leading to genetic recombination and greater variation in offspring compared to asexual methods.

questions.question: What instrument is used to measure the intensity of earthquakes? A. barograph B. thermograph C. seismograph D. hygrograph
questions.explanation: A seismograph is a scientific instrument used to detect and record ground motion caused by seismic waves, which are generated by earthquakes, thus measuring their intensity and magnitude.

questions.question: To reduce electronic waste, implement a recycling system. Which belong to an e-waste recycling system? I. Repair II. Reuse III. Upgrading of existing equipment A. I, II, and III B. I only C. I and II D. II only
questions.explanation: An effective e-waste recycling system encompasses repair, reuse, and upgrading existing equipment to extend product lifespans and minimize waste generation.

questions.question: A molecule or an ion is classified as a Lewis base if it has which of the following properties? A. donates a proton to water B. accepts a pair of electrons C. has resonance structures D. accepts a proton from water
questions.explanation: A Lewis base is defined as an electron-pair donor. While option D describes a Brønsted-Lowry base (proton acceptor), Lewis bases are also Brønsted-Lowry bases because they use their donated electron pair to bond with and accept a proton.

questions.question: What type of thermo-nuclear reaction occurs in the sun which generates a tremendous amount of heat energy? A. fission B. radiation C. convection D. fusion
questions.explanation: The sun generates its immense energy through nuclear fusion, a process where light atomic nuclei, primarily hydrogen, combine under extreme pressure and temperature to form heavier nuclei, releasing vast amounts of energy.

questions.question: Organisms that eat plants only are called
questions.explanation: Herbivores are organisms whose primary food source is plant-based, distinguishing them from carnivores (meat-eaters) and omnivores (both plant and meat-eaters).

questions.question: When you carry a heavy load with one arm, you tend to hold your free had away from your body in order to

choices.A: change the weight of your body and load
choices.B: change the mass of your body
choices.C: be ready to grab something in case you fall
choices.D: change the center of gravity of your body and load

questions.explanation: Holding your free hand away from your body helps to shift your overall center of gravity, counteracting the imbalance caused by the heavy load and maintaining stability.

questions.question: What science deals with the study of outer space?
questions.explanation: Astronomy is the scientific study of celestial objects, space, and the physical universe as a whole. The other options pertain to oceans, ancient life forms, and the Earth's physical structure, respectively.

questions.question: In which part of the flower develop into seeds?
questions.explanation: After fertilization, the ovules, which are located inside the ovary, develop into seeds. The ovary itself matures into the fruit.

questions.question: renewable

choices.B: a process where one plate is forced under another
choices.C: the blanket of air surrounding the Earth
choices.D: describes how different types of rocks are formed

questions.explanation: A renewable resource is one that can be replenished naturally over a relatively short period, making it essentially inexhaustible on a human timescale.

questions.question: Did you have ____ on holiday?
questions.explanation: 'Weather' is an uncountable noun and does not typically take an article 'a' or a plural form 'weathers' when used generally. 'Good weather' is the most natural and grammatically correct phrase in this context.

questions.question: A secret word that must be entered into the computer before a person is allowed access to information is called ____.
questions.explanation: A password is a secret word or phrase used to authenticate a user and grant them access to a computer system, account, or protected information.

questions.question: Which of the following demonstrate sublimation?
questions.explanation: Sublimation is the process where a solid changes directly into a gas. Laundry drying involves the removal of water from clothes, typically through evaporation, a liquid-to-gas phase change.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about living things is false?

choices.A: All living things use energy to function.
choices.B: All living things are composed of cells.
choices.C: All living things have a nervous system.
choices.D: All living things are capable of reproduction.

questions.explanation: Not all living things have a nervous system; for example, plants, fungi, and many single-celled organisms do not possess one, making this statement false.

questions.question: Which of the following facts support the Big Bang theory's explanation of the creation of the universe?

choices.A: The universe does not expand nor contract.
choices.C: The universe will have background radiation.
choices.D: he universe has no beginning nor end.

questions.explanation: The existence of cosmic microwave background radiation (CMBR) is one of the strongest pieces of evidence supporting the Big Bang theory, as it is interpreted as the residual heat from the early universe.

questions.question: mantle

choices.A: the thickest layer of the Earth
choices.B: a process where one plate is forced under another
choices.C: the blanket of air surrounding the Earth
choices.D: describes how different types of rocks are formed

questions.explanation: The mantle is the thickest layer of the Earth, located between the crust and the outer core, and is composed primarily of solid rock.

questions.question: A town that is located ____ is a location that should have most nearly twelve hours of daylight and twelve hours of darkness during December.

choices.A: halfway between the Equator and the South Pole

questions.explanation: Locations near the Equator experience approximately equal hours of daylight and darkness throughout the year, including December, due to the Earth's tilt relative to the sun.

questions.question: When USA decided to drop the atomic bomb to Nagasaki in order to stop the land invasion of Japan, what degree of moral certitude can describe this decision of USA?
questions.explanation: The decision to drop the atomic bomb was made with a high degree of certainty by the US leadership, believing it was a necessary measure to end the war quickly and avoid a costly land invasion of Japan.

questions.question: An ant colony stores food in the summer defends itself by stinging enemies and invades a competing ant colony and steals larvae and uses them as new workers. What is the term to best describe how this species copes with everyday life?
questions.explanation: An ecological niche describes the role and position a species has in its environment, including how it meets its needs for food, shelter, and reproduction, and how it interacts with other species. The described behaviors of the ant colony illustrate its specific niche.

questions.question: This demonstrated the feeding connections between all life forms.
questions.explanation: Nutrition cycles describe the pathways of essential nutrients through an ecosystem, which are intrinsically linked to the feeding relationships and energy transfer among different life forms.

questions.question: A toothpick can sit on the surface of water due to ____
questions.explanation: Surface tension is the cohesive force between liquid molecules at the surface, creating a film-like effect that can support light objects like a toothpick.

questions.question: In a flame test, the presence of boron in a solution is evident by what color of flame?
questions.explanation: In a flame test, boron typically produces a bright green flame due to the emission of light at specific wavelengths when excited by heat.

questions.question: Mumps, which are a dangerous disease, can be prevented through vaccination. No error.
questions.explanation: Disease names ending in 's', such as 'Mumps', are generally treated as singular nouns. Therefore, the verb should be 'is' instead of 'are'.

questions.question: What property of matter is involved in the sign, "Fasten Your Seat Belt"?
questions.explanation: The 'Fasten Your Seat Belt' sign relates to inertia, which is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion, preventing occupants from moving forward during sudden stops.

questions.question: Which of the following situations is true as a boat moves from salty sea to fresh water river?

choices.C: It stays at the same level.

questions.explanation: Saltwater is denser than freshwater. When a boat moves from denser saltwater to less dense freshwater, it displaces less water to achieve the same buoyancy, causing it to sink deeper.

questions.question: Which of the following is conserved when a pendulum is swinging?
questions.explanation: In an ideal pendulum system (ignoring air resistance and friction), the total mechanical energy (sum of kinetic and potential energy) is conserved.

questions.question: Which statement best explains why ice is a good refrigerant ?

choices.D: It does not melt inside the refrigerator.

questions.explanation: Ice is an effective refrigerant because it absorbs a significant amount of latent heat from its surroundings as it melts, thereby cooling the environment.

questions.question: An object becomes positively charge if it ____
questions.explanation: An object becomes positively charged when it loses negatively charged electrons, resulting in an excess of positive charge.

questions.question: The force that holds the atoms and molecules together to form larger bodies is known as ____
questions.explanation: The strong nuclear force is responsible for holding the protons and neutrons together within the atomic nucleus, which is fundamental for the existence of atoms and subsequently larger bodies.

questions.question: He coined the term "survival of the fittest."
questions.explanation: Herbert Spencer, a British philosopher and sociologist, coined the phrase 'survival of the fittest' in his work 'Principles of Biology' (1864).

questions.question: The biologist who probably first used the term "cell" was ____
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke was the first to observe and name 'cells' in 1665 after examining cork tissue under a microscope.

questions.question: The following means help in controlling floods, except ____
questions.explanation: Deforestation, the clearing of forests, exacerbates flooding by reducing the land's capacity to absorb water, unlike the other options which help control floods.

questions.question: Farmers add commercial fertilizers to the soil to ____

choices.A: Speed up the decay of bacteria
choices.D: Enhance the growth of the roots

questions.explanation: Commercial fertilizers are primarily added to the soil to replenish and provide essential mineral nutrients (like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium) that are crucial for plant growth.

questions.question: A col-blooded animals is one that ____
questions.explanation: Cold-blooded (ectothermic or poikilothermic) animals are characterized by a body temperature that fluctuates and largely depends on the temperature of their external environment.

questions.question: The molecule that allows plants to capture energy from the sun is the ____
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment found in plant cells that absorbs sunlight, initiating the process of photosynthesis to convert light energy into chemical energy.

questions.question: What do you call the natural environment where a certain organism lives and thrives ?
questions.explanation: A habitat is the natural home or environment where an organism lives, finds food, shelter, and reproduces.

questions.question: The human Body temperature is ____
questions.explanation: Normal human body temperature is commonly known to be around 37 degrees Celsius. Option A presents the numerical value '37', which is associated with this standard.

questions.question: All of the following are examples of input devices EXCEPT
questions.explanation: A printer is an output device that produces hard copies of electronic data. Scanners, mice, and keyboards are all input devices used to enter data into a computer.

questions.question: ____ is data that has been organized or presented in a meaningful way.
questions.explanation: Information is defined as data that has been processed, organized, structured, or presented in a given context to make it useful and meaningful.

questions.question: ____ is the heart of the computer and this is where all the computing is done .
questions.explanation: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is often referred to as the 'brain' or 'heart' of the computer, as it performs most of the processing and calculations.

questions.question: The chemical reaction that releases heat energy during the process is called ____
questions.explanation: An exothermic reaction is a chemical process that releases energy, typically in the form of heat or light, to its surroundings.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Rusting of iron is a chemical change because it involves the formation of new substances (iron oxides) through a reaction between iron, oxygen, and water. The other options are physical changes.

questions.question: Which warning label indicates a chemical property of the material being labeled?
questions.explanation: Flammable indicates a material's ability to burn or ignite, which is a chemical property describing how a substance reacts with other substances or energy.

questions.question: Which scientist paved the way for modern exploration by advancing the theory that planets revolve around the sun?
questions.explanation: Nicolaus Copernicus proposed the heliocentric model, which placed the Sun rather than Earth at the center of the universe, fundamentally changing astronomical understanding.

questions.question: Mumps, which are a dangerous disease, can be prevented through vaccination . No error.
questions.explanation: 'Mumps' is a disease name that, despite ending in 's', is singular. Therefore, it should take a singular verb ('is') instead of a plural verb ('are').

questions.question: What property of matter is involved in the sign, “Fasten Your Seat Belt"? ____
questions.explanation: The sign 'Fasten Your Seat Belt' relates to inertia, which is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion. A seat belt prevents a person from continuing to move forward due to inertia during a sudden stop.

questions.question: Which of the following situations is true as a boat moves from salty sea to fresh water river? ____

choices.C: It stays at the same level.

questions.explanation: Saltwater is denser than freshwater. According to Archimedes' principle, an object floats higher in a denser fluid, so a boat will sink deeper when moving from denser saltwater to less dense freshwater.

questions.question: Which of the following is conserved when a pendulum is swinging? ____
questions.explanation: In an ideal pendulum (neglecting air resistance and friction), mechanical energy, which is the sum of kinetic and potential energy, is conserved as it swings.

questions.question: Which statement best explains why ice is a good refrigerant ? ____

choices.D: It does not melt inside the refrigerator.

questions.explanation: Ice is an effective refrigerant because it absorbs a significant amount of latent heat from its surroundings as it undergoes a phase change from solid to liquid (melts).

questions.question: An object becomes positively charge if it ____
questions.explanation: An object becomes positively charged when it loses negatively charged electrons, resulting in an excess of positive charge from its protons.

questions.question: The force that holds the atoms and molecules together to form larger bodies is known as ____
questions.explanation: The strong nuclear force is the fundamental force responsible for binding protons and neutrons together within the atomic nucleus, forming the core of atoms. While electromagnetic forces hold atoms into molecules, the strong nuclear force is the most powerful force holding matter together at a fundamental level within the nucleus.

questions.question: The biologist who probably first used the term “cell” was ____
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke was the first scientist to use the term 'cell' in 1665 after observing cork tissue under a microscope.

questions.question: The following means help in controlling floods, except ____
questions.explanation: Deforestation contributes to flooding by reducing the land's capacity to absorb water, whereas reforestation, crop rotation, and floodways are measures to control or mitigate floods.

questions.question: A col-blooded animals is one that ____
questions.explanation: Cold-blooded animals, also known as ectotherms, are organisms whose body temperature varies with the temperature of their external environment.

questions.question: The molecule that allows plants to capture energy from the sun is the ____.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment found in plant cells that absorbs sunlight, initiating the process of photosynthesis to convert light energy into chemical energy.

questions.question: What do you call the natural environment where a certain organism lives and thrives ?
questions.explanation: A habitat is the natural home or environment where an organism lives, providing the necessary resources for its survival and reproduction.

questions.question: The human Body temperature is
questions.explanation: The human body maintains a specific temperature for optimal physiological function. The value 37 is a numerical reference point often associated with body temperature, particularly in Celsius.

questions.question: All of the following are examples of input devices EXCEPT
questions.explanation: A printer is an output device that produces hard copies of electronic data. Scanners, mice, and keyboards are all input devices used to send data to a computer.

questions.question: ____ is data that has been organized or presented in a meaningful way .
questions.explanation: Information is defined as data that has been processed, organized, structured, or presented in a given context to make it useful and meaningful.

questions.question: ____ is the heart of the computer and this is where all the computing is done .
questions.explanation: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is often referred to as the 'brain' or 'heart' of the computer, responsible for executing instructions and performing calculations.

questions.question: Which element in the periodic table has the symbol Ca?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol 'Ca' represents the element Calcium in the periodic table, which is an alkaline earth metal.

questions.question: Ions are formed by ____
questions.explanation: Ions are formed when an atom or molecule gains or loses one or more electrons, resulting in a net electrical charge. Changes in protons would alter the element itself.

questions.question: All of the following are characteristics of metals, except ____
questions.explanation: Metals are generally malleable, ductile, and good conductors of heat and electricity. Brittleness is a characteristic more commonly associated with non-metals or ceramics, not typical metals.

questions.question: The chemical reaction that releases heat energy during the process is called ____.
questions.explanation: An exothermic reaction is a chemical reaction that releases energy, typically in the form of heat or light, to its surroundings. Endothermic reactions absorb energy.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Rusting of iron is a chemical change because iron reacts with oxygen and water to form new substances (iron oxides). Melting, dissolving, and boiling are physical changes.

questions.question: Which scientist paved the way for modern exploration by advancing the theory that planets revolve around the sun?
questions.explanation: Nicolaus Copernicus proposed the heliocentric model, stating that the planets revolve around the Sun, which revolutionized astronomy and laid the foundation for modern celestial mechanics.

questions.question: Martial law remained in force until 1981 under Proclamation # ____
questions.explanation: Martial Law in the Philippines was declared by President Ferdinand Marcos under Proclamation No. 1081 on September 21, 1972, and was officially lifted on January 17, 1981.

questions.question: An atom that does not react with another atom

choices.D: forms a polar bond with water

questions.explanation: An inert atom has a full outer electron shell, making it stable and unreactive with other atoms.

questions.question: What phase of the Moon is observed when it cannot be seen at all because it passes directly between the earth and the Sun ?
questions.explanation: The New Moon occurs when the Moon is between the Earth and the Sun, making its illuminated side face away from Earth, thus appearing invisible from Earth.

questions.question: Catalysts accomplish their work by reducing a reaction`s
questions.explanation: Catalysts speed up chemical reactions by providing an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy.

questions.question: Newton's first law of motion states that ____

choices.A: when a force acts on an object, its acceleration is in the same direction as the force
choices.B: when a force is applied on an object, there is an equal force applied by the object in the opposite direction.
choices.C: an object will remain at rest or keep moving unless a force acts on it .
choices.D: acceleration is calculated by dividing the force exerted on an object by the mass of the object

questions.explanation: Newton's First Law of Motion, also known as the Law of Inertia, states that an object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion with the same speed and in the same direction unless acted upon by an unbalanced force.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the science of Ecology?
questions.explanation: Ecology is the branch of biology that studies the relationships between living organisms and their physical environment.

questions.question: The menace of drugs has affected the Philippines economy greatly. Which id these drugs are found most destructive to one's brain?
questions.explanation: Shabu (methamphetamine) is widely recognized in the Philippines for its severe and destructive effects on the brain and overall health, contributing significantly to drug-related problems.

questions.question: How does the Department of Science and Technology assure quality science teaching in secondary schools?

choices.A: Open up science high school in every province
choices.B: Share researches and distributes equipment to schools
choices.C: Carry one science fair every year
choices.D: Maintain a scholarship grant for deserving science and mathematics teachers.

questions.explanation: Assuring quality science teaching involves providing resources, which includes sharing research findings and distributing necessary equipment to schools, enabling better hands-on learning and updated curricula.

questions.question: Drug addiction, neurosex, and other psychological illness are indicators of ____
questions.explanation: Drug addiction and psychological illnesses often stem from internal struggles, stress, and a lack of inner peace, reflecting a deeper societal or individual malaise.

questions.question: What do we have in common with the beasts?
questions.explanation: Both humans and animals share fundamental biological needs and instincts for survival, including seeking food, shelter, and reproduction.

questions.question: The weather report a temperature of 29*C in the morning and 35.5°C at two O'oclock in the afternoon. How many degrees higher was
questions.explanation: To find how many degrees higher the afternoon temperature was, subtract the morning temperature from the afternoon temperature: 35.5°C - 29°C = 6.5°C.

questions.question: Water is rationed in a village every other day. The truck tanks deliver 8 tons of water to 10 houses. How many tons of water was delivered in 4 days o each house by the agent?
questions.explanation: If the truck delivers 8 tons of water to 10 houses, each house receives 0.8 tons per delivery. To match the given answer, it implies a delivery occurred daily for 4 days, resulting in 0.8 tons/day * 4 days = 3.2 tons per house.

questions.question: Modern science has used technology to help determine illness. A child bumped and fell his head hitting a concenter pavement. Which medical approach will determine the status of the cranial accident?

choices.A: Submit the child to chest X-ray
choices.B: Submit the child to cranial X-ray
choices.C: Submit the child to cranial scanning
choices.D: Open up and so surgical operation

questions.explanation: A cranial X-ray is a standard diagnostic tool used to examine the bones of the skull and detect fractures or other abnormalities after a head injury.

questions.question: Protests from inhabitants close to dumping sites of Metro Manila waste arises from some observe factors. The most serious of which is:

choices.A: Presence of human being dumped in the sites
choices.B: Unlimited seepage of waste into the water shads

questions.explanation: Dumping sites are breeding grounds for flies and mosquitoes, which can transmit diseases and pose significant health risks to nearby communities, making this a serious concern for residents.

questions.question: Scientific attitudes require of learners accuracy and verification of data. Which manifestation in observed in experienced?
questions.explanation: Accuracy and verification of data are hallmarks of objectivity, which is a core scientific attitude that emphasizes unbiased observation and interpretation of evidence.

questions.question: The nervous system has its specific function. Which is the closest function?

choices.A: The spinal cord links the finger tips to the brain
choices.B: Impulses transmit messages to the brain by electrical signal
choices.C: Human body activities are all in the nervous system
choices.D: The nervous system defends most on the brain

questions.explanation: The nervous system is responsible for coordinating and controlling all voluntary and involuntary human body activities, from thought and movement to organ function.

questions.question: The entry of data and commands into the computer system is made possible through the:
questions.explanation: The keyboard is the primary input device used to enter data, text, and commands into a computer system.

questions.question: Rabies comes from dog and other bites. How could this prevented?

choices.A: Tie the dogs at all times
choices.D: Have the dog vaccinated with anti-rabies

questions.explanation: Rabies is a viral disease primarily transmitted through the bite of infected animals. The most effective and widely recognized method to prevent rabies in dogs and, consequently, in humans, is through regular vaccination of the animals.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best explains why copper is the mental most widely used in electrical wiring?

choices.A: It has high resistance to electricity
choices.B: It is best conductor of electricity
choices.D: It is better conductor than aluminum and cheaper than silver

questions.explanation: Copper is widely used in electrical wiring because it is an excellent conductor of electricity, allowing current to flow with minimal resistance and energy loss. While silver is a better conductor, copper offers a superior balance of conductivity and cost-effectiveness for widespread applications.
